Event: Leads the teachers' strike to get higher-quality education
Explanation: She risks conflict to improve conditions and education quality, benefiting herself and students.

Event: Bonds with Bart more than either would like
Explanation: Unwanted closeness still yields mutual understanding and some emotional benefit.

Event: Bonds with Edna more than either would like
Explanation: Bart gains a caring adult ally despite reluctance, and Edna gains connection with a difficult student.

Event: Continues teaching despite burnout, preferring it over other work
Explanation: She chooses meaningful work that sustains her ideals and benefits students’ learning.

Event: Feels students are doomed regardless of budget
Explanation: Pessimism leads to low investment that hurts students while doing little to help him.

Event: Develops a secret romance with Skinner
Explanation: Both gain intimacy and support, with no clear harm to others in the text.

Event: Develops a secret romance with Edna
Explanation: He gains companionship and she gains affection, with neutral outside effects.

Event: Breaks up with Skinner
Explanation: The breakup removes mutual support without any shown compensating benefit.

Event: Marries Ned Flanders
Explanation: Both gain stable companionship and emotional fulfillment.

Event: Pretty much forgets about Seymour after marrying Ned
Explanation: Moving on aids her emotional closure while leaving Seymour sidelined.

Event: Performs a striptease balloon act at a talent show
Explanation: She gets attention/fun while strongly disturbing Bart and shocking the crowd.

Event: Enjoys watching Edna’s striptease act
Explanation: They gain entertainment while reinforcing a show that disturbs others.

Event: Eats an armful of unpaid concessions
Explanation: He gains food without paying, directly harming the vendor.

Event: Leaves Edna for the marriage counselor
Explanation: He upgrades his relationship at severe emotional cost to Edna.

Event: Does not kill herself or quit despite misery
Explanation: She preserves her life and career, allowing ongoing benefit to herself and students.

Event: Single outs Bart to keep her sanity while teaching
Explanation: Focusing discipline on Bart helps her cope but increases his burden.

Event: Makes efforts to connect with Bart
Explanation: She nurtures a troubled student and strengthens their relationship.

Event: Tries to do a little better for her sake
Explanation: He improves his behavior/performance, helping himself and pleasing his teacher.

Event: Habitually responds with dry, sarcastic remarks
Explanation: Sarcasm vents her frustration but strains relationships without clear gain.

Event: Changes Edna’s last name in the Spanish dub
Explanation: The pun-preserving name improves comprehension and humor with minimal cost.

Event: Spies on Sideshow Bob’s date with Edna
Explanation: He ruins their date mainly out of antagonism, gaining little beyond momentary satisfaction.

Event: Feels outraged that Bart ruined his one chance with Edna
Explanation: Rage harms his mood and stability without improving his situation.

Event: Flirts with or is attracted to Edna (Patty and other women)
Explanation: Mutual attraction offers potential connection with no stated harm.

Event: Marries Ned and becomes mostly “Ned’s wife”
Explanation: She gains security and affection, with only a loss of narrative individuality.

Event: Emotionally supports and helps Edna in her hardships
Explanation: He sacrifices mischief time to comfort her, benefiting both.

Event: Terrorizes Edna “on principle”
Explanation: He enjoys mischief and status while making her life harder.

Event: Cares about students’ education more than other teachers
Explanation: Her concern drives better teaching and personal meaning.

Event: Implements policies/attitude that doom students regardless of budget
Explanation: His fatalism undercuts improvements without improving his own lot.

Event: States Bart is “the closest thing to a man” in her life
Explanation: This reveals unhealthy loneliness and unsettles Bart, with no benefit.

Event: Double dates with 18-year-old Bart and Homer (Edna willing)
Explanation: She seeks companionship from a dubious situation that mostly benefits her.

Event: Politely rejects dating Edna
Explanation: He avoids a bad match while Edna is nudged toward healthier relationships.

Event: Encourages Bart that both can do better with other people
Explanation: She reframes the rejection positively, helping them both aim higher.

Event: Accuses Edna of dating Bart
Explanation: The baseless accusation strains relations without clear gain.

Event: Replies sarcastically that she avoids Bart because he’s “so delicious”
Explanation: The sarcasm deflects the accusation and undercuts Cantwell’s hostility without serious harm.

Event: Tries to seduce Mr. Bergstrom
Explanation: She pursues mutual adult attraction with limited collateral effects.

Event: Finds a provocative picture of Edna and decides to attend Parents’ Night
Explanation: He chases personal titillation while risking awkwardness and objectifying her.

Event: Wears college-style clothes and acts younger to recapture the past
Explanation: Nostalgia gives fleeting relief but deepens her sense of lost time.

Event: Gives Bart extra credit so he passes fourth grade
Explanation: She rewards genuine effort, preserving his progress and her standards.

Event: Studies hard and still initially fails his final test
Explanation: His effort benefits Edna’s perception and fairness more than his grade until the extra credit.

Event: Reveals in her writings that she believes in Bart’s potential
Explanation: Her faith posthumously supports his self-image and honors her own values.

Event: Starts drinking her days away after divorce and job loss
Explanation: Alcohol coping devastates her well-being while only mildly easing others from her intensity.

Event: Gets fired from a prestigious private school
Explanation: Losing a good job hurts her prospects; others get a calmer environment.

Event: Takes a job at Springfield Elementary
Explanation: She regains employment and provides one of the only caring teachers there.

Event: Frequently drinks alone (“Dinner for one, wine for three…”)
Explanation: Heavy solitary drinking self-harms while only marginally numbing pain.

Event: Accepts and laughs at the “crab apple” name joke
Explanation: Leaning into the joke diffuses mockery and smooths social tension.

Event: Engages in terrible dating life and quick flings
Explanation: Short flings rarely satisfy her deeper need for love and stability.

Event: Hooks up with many people before marrying Ned
Explanation: Frequent hookups offer fleeting comfort but deepen Ned’s doubts and her insecurity.

Event: Marries Ned despite his worries about her past
Explanation: He chooses forgiveness and love, gaining a partner and blessing her with acceptance.

Event: Torments Bart as his sitcom arch-nemesis
Explanation: She retaliates to preserve order and vent frustration, increasing his suffering.

Event: Torments Edna as her sitcom arch-nemesis
Explanation: His pranks amuse him and fulfill his role while making her life harder.

Event: Treats Bart as a worthy opponent with mutual respect
Explanation: Respect turns conflict into growth for both.

Event: Treats Edna as a worthy opponent with mutual respect
Explanation: Bart gains guidance and challenge while honoring her as a rival he values.
